sit: You catch a pass in the slot, 15 feet from the net. The goalie is leaning slightly right. You have a clear shooting lane to the left. What should you do?
opts: Pass out to a teammate on the perimeter
Shoot quickly to the left where the goalie is out of position
Stickhandle and wait for a much better angle
Turn and skate back out

sit: You lose a faceoff in your own zone. The other team's center has the puck. What's your immediate priority?
opts: Argue with the referee
Go pressure the puck carrier immediately
Skate back to the bench
Stand still and watch the play unfold

sit: You're a defender and an opposing player is skating toward you with the puck, 20 feet away. What's right?
opts: Skate straight at them as fast as you can and try to knock the puck loose
Back up and maintain a steady gap while angling them toward the boards
Turn around and skate backward immediately all the way back to your crease
Stand still and hold your ground

sit: Your defenseman has the puck behind the net. Both wingers are covered. The other defenseman is open on the far side. What's the best option?
opts: Force a pass to a covered winger anyway
Walk out from behind the net alone and skate
Pass across to the open defenseman to reset the breakout
Shoot the puck hard around the boards and change

sit: Your team just got the puck in your own zone. The other team's forecheckers are still in your end. What do you do with the puck?
opts: Shoot it down the ice to the far end as fast as you can
Skate it out calmly — the forecheckers are out of position and you have time
Wait behind your own net for the forecheckers to get tired and regroup
Pass it back to your goalie and let them freeze it for a faceoff

sit: You're in the offensive zone with the puck. You have a shooting lane but your teammate is streaking to the net completely unmarked. The goalie hasn't seen them yet. What do you do?
opts: Shoot — you already have the lane open
Pass to your unmarked teammate cutting to the net
Dump the puck into the corner
Hold the puck and stickhandle to wait for a better play

sit: You're the lone defender on a 2-on-1 against you. The puck carrier has a teammate to their right. What's the most important thing you do?
opts: Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to poke the puck away before the pass can be made
Take away the pass — stay in the middle, force the shooter, and let the goalie handle the shot
Chase the trailing player and leave
Back up all the way to your own crease and let the goalie handle the two attackers alone

sit: Your team is forechecking. Two forwards are already deep in the offensive zone. You're the third forward. Where do you set up?
opts: Skate deep into the zone with the other two forwards and forecheck hard
Stay at the top of the zone near the blue line as the safety
Head to the bench for a quick change
Skate down to the half-wall spot

sit: Your team just won a puck battle in the corner in your own zone. The other team has four players still deep in your end. You have speed. What's the call?
opts: Reset and regroup behind your own net first
Go — skate it out fast and attack while they're out of position
Pass to your D and let them carry it up the ice and decide
Shoot it down the ice and chase

sit: You have the puck behind your own net. A forechecker is coming at you hard. You have a defenseman open to your left. What do you do?
opts: Try to outskate the forechecker and carry it yourself up the wall fast
Quickly pass to the open defenseman before the forechecker arrives
Stop behind the net and wait it out
Shoot the puck down the ice yourself

sit: You have the puck near the goal line, way out to the side. The goalie has the near post covered completely. A teammate is at the far post. What do you do?
opts: Shoot from where you are — any shot on net is a good shot from this angle for sure
Pass across to your teammate at the far post — the goalie can't get there in time
Skate to center ice and take the shot
Dump the puck behind the net and go fight for it in the corner with your teammate

sit: An attacker is carrying the puck along the right boards toward your net. You're the defender. What's the goal of your positioning?
opts: Stop them cold by skating straight at them and poking the puck off their stick quickly
Angle your body to steer them along the boards and away from the middle of the ice
Back up all the way to your crease and let the goalie handle it
Wait for them to shoot from a bad angle and then block the shot with your body fast

sit: Your D picks up the puck. The strong-side winger is covered. The weak-side winger is open on the far side. What's the breakout call?
opts: Force the pass to the covered strong-side winger and hope they win it
Pass to the open weak-side winger — they're the clean option
Skate it out yourself
Rim the puck around the boards to the far-side corner area

sit: An attacker is rushing the puck at you at full speed. You're the only defender. The attacker is fast. What do you do?
opts: Charge at them at full speed to meet them until someone tells you otherwise
Back up at a controlled pace
Turn around and race them to your net
Stand completely

sit: You're on the power play at the half-wall. The puck carrier passes it to you in stride. The goalie is leaning to their left. What's the right play?
opts: Stop the puck first and then look around carefully for a good pass
One-time it to the right side where the goalie hasn't moved yet
Stickhandle and wait
Pass it back up to the point

sit: The puck turns over in the neutral zone and now the other team has a 2-on-2 going toward your net. You're one of the two defenders. What's your priority?
opts: Both defenders chase the puck carrier hard and try to strip the puck before they get a shot off
Take your side — one defender takes the carrier, the other takes the second attacker — and protect the middle
Both defenders collapse down into the crease and pack the net to block every shot attempt
Skate straight back to the goal line and let the goalie handle the 2-on-2 rush on their own

sit: Your team is entering the offensive zone. The defender is set at their blue line — they're not going to let you carry it in. What's the best entry option?
opts: Carry the puck in at full speed and try to blow right past the defender with pure speed
Chip the puck into the zone behind the defender and skate hard to get it first
Stop at the blue line and wait for your teammates
Pass it back to the D and regroup

sit: Your teammate just won a puck battle in the corner in the defensive zone. They have the puck but defenders are still around them. You're nearby without a defender on you. What do you do?
opts: Skate away to center ice
Get close enough to be an easy pass — support behind or beside them
Call for a long stretch pass up to the blue line and break out fast
Stand still and watch the battle play out for yourself

sit: You're the puck carrier on a 2-on-1. The lone defender commits fully to your teammate. The goalie slides across expecting your pass. What's the right call now?
opts: Pass to your teammate just as planned — stick with it
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass back to the other side again
Pull up and regroup back

sit: Your team turned the puck over and now three attackers are coming at your two defensemen. You're the left D. The puck carrier is on your side. What's your job?
opts: Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to strip the puck before they get into your zone
Take your side and force the carrier wide — your partner protects the middle attacker
Chase the trailing attacker down the far side and leave the puck carrier to your partner
Back up all the way to your crease

sit: You're about to receive a pass in the offensive zone. Before the puck reaches you, what should you already be doing?
opts: Waiting with your stick flat on the ice, ready to catch and control the puck cleanly on contact
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so your teammate knows you are ready
Watching the puck travel the whole way to make sure that you don't miss it when it arrives

sit: Your team is on the power play. The penalty kill gets the puck and two players immediately break the other way 2-on-2. What should your team do?
opts: Chase the puck carrier with everyone and try to strip the puck back fast
One forward pressures the puck, the others recover their positions — don't panic
All five skaters retreat to your own zone
Ignore it — you'll get the puck back soon

sit: Your team has a power play. You're standing in front of the goalie. Your teammate at the point winds up for a shot. What's your job?
opts: Skate out of the way so the goalie can see the puck clearly and make an easy save there
Stay planted and block the goalie's sightline — screen them, and be ready to tip the shot
Skate to the corner to get position early for the rebound off the boards
Call loudly for a pass instead of the shot so you can score from a better angle

sit: You're the right defenseman covering an attacker on your side. Another attacker cuts across in front of the net toward your side. Your partner calls 'switch!' What do you do?
opts: Stay with your original assignment and completely ignore the switch call from your partner
Take the attacker cutting to your side, let your partner recover their original man
Call the referee for a penalty
Back up all the way to the crease and try to cover both of the attackers at once

sit: Your team is under heavy forecheck pressure. Your D picks up the puck behind your net. Two forecheckers are pinching in from both sides. What breakout option gives you the best chance?
opts: Wheel around the net and try to carry the puck out all by yourself straight through the pressure up the middle of the ice
Reverse the puck to the other side — one forechecker can't follow, buying time for your winger to get position
Rim it hard around the boards
Shoot it down the ice as far as you can for icing to get a whistle and a fresh faceoff in the defensive zone right now

sit: The other team is sitting back in a 1-2-2 neutral zone setup. Every time your team tries to enter, they get stopped at the red line. What adjustment do you make?
opts: Keep carrying the puck in at full speed until the trap finally breaks down and lets you by
Pull the puck back to your defensemen, use a D-to-D pass to overload one side, or try a stretch pass to a breaking forward
Dump the puck deep every single rush and grind them down with a heavy forecheck eventually
Skate around the outside of the trap along the boards and curl back into the middle with speed

sit: Your team is taking a defensive zone faceoff. Before the puck drops, what should every player already know?
opts: Nothing at all — players should just react to where the puck goes after the official drops it
Their assignment — who they're covering and where they're going based on whether you win or lose the draw
Where the nearest door to the bench is in case their shift ends right after the puck drops
To skate as fast as they possibly can toward the puck the moment it hits the ice surface

sit: An opposing winger has broken the puck out of their own zone and is coming at you with speed. You're at the red line. The attacker is fast and has a step on you. What's your priority?
opts: Charge right at them and try to take the puck off their stick before they can get across the red line and into your zone
Back up at a pace that doesn't let them get speed on you, angle them toward the boards, and wait
Turn around and race them straight back to your net to try to beat them to the front before they can fire off a shot
Stand completely still at the red line and wait for them to come to you before you commit to any kind of defensive move
